description: Video-based, screen-share style reviews of Colosseum project homepages. Focuses heavily on UX clarity, trust signals, product messaging, technical depth, and builder polish.
overall_judging_style: Product-first, brutally honest but encouraging. Prioritizes: 1) Immediate clarity (who is this for?), 2) Trust & institutional signals, 3) Technical/demo depth (contracts, docs, GitHub), 4) Visual polish without overdone Web3 aesthetics, 5) Actionable next steps (waitlist, docs, faucet, API).

reviews.key_feedback: Strong creator-business-in-one-link concept but confusing (social network? Linktree? Super-app?). Loves mobile preview, revenue streams, on-chain analytics ($24.5K example), pricing tiers. Critiques: overdone network background graphic, unclear core value prop, too many features at once.
reviews.style_notes: Emphasizes Web3-native creators but warns against limiting audience.

reviews.key_feedback: Excellent UGC marketplace for creators/brands (on-chain reputation that follows you). Clear 4-step flow (Discover → Agree → Deliver → Rate). Loves Solana-secured payments and reputation scoreboard. Very clean and simple.
reviews.style_notes: Highlights simplicity and no-middleman value for creators/brands.

reviews.key_feedback: Strong stablecoin landing page selling trust/privacy/guardrails. Simple hero + clear benefits (earn by holding, zero-interest borrow, private when you want). Docs section is solid for institutions. Wants more: contract address, faucet, API exploration.
critiques: Docs could go deeper (faucet, contract, API)
Needs more hands-on exploration for coders
reviews.style_notes: Stables = selling trust. Targets compliance officers/fund managers.

reviews.key_feedback: Clean MEV redistribution page (trader > bot). Strong tagline, sealed-bid auction mechanics, stats, comparison table. Good for traders. Minor nitpicks on broken links and missing README/GitHub depth.
reviews.style_notes: Appreciates technical depth and trader-focused messaging.

extracted_patterns_for_super_agent: Gui judges landing pages on: clarity (who + what in <5s), trust signals (especially stables/RWA), visual polish without clichés, technical accessibility (docs/contracts/GitHub), and actionable CTAs. He rewards simplicity and builder polish; penalizes feature overload and vague Web3 aesthetics.
